arts.ttl: Bournemouth vs Manchester City - FA Cup: Live score, team news and updates as the two Premier League sides meet for the first time in the competition
arts.sum: Follow Mail Sport's live blog for the latest score, team news and updates as Bournemouth host Manchester City in the quarter-finals of the FA Cup at Vitality Stadium.

arts.ttl: FA Cup semi-finals: Bournemouth or City to play Forest as Palace face Villa
arts.sum: The winners of the FA Cup quarter-final between Bournemouth and Manchester City will face Nottingham Forest in the last four, while Crystal Palace will play Aston Villa in the semi-finals.

arts.ttl: Why Savinho misses Bournemouth game amid Man City injury issues
arts.sum: Manchester City winger Savinho was not part of the squad to take on Bournemouth in an important FA Cup quarter-final

arts.ttl: FA Cup semi-final draw: Manchester City, Nottingham Forest, Aston Villa, Bournemouth, Crystal Palace learn last-four opponents
arts.sum: The remaining five Premier League teams left in the FA Cup learn their semi-final opponents ahead of their Wembley Stadium dates next month.

arts.ttl: Forest draw Bournemouth or Man City, Palace v Villa
arts.sum: The full FA Cup semi-final draw, with ties taking place on the weekend of Saturday, 26 April.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth vs Man City: Confirmed Lineups, Team News and Score Prediction

arts.ttl: Man City face Premier League fixture change as race for Champions League takes twist
arts.sum: Manchester City's Premier League game with Aston Villa next month will be rearranged

arts.ttl: Manchester United 'join race' to sign Austrian wonderkid dubbed 'next Erling Haaland'
arts.sum: Manchester United are reportedly in the hunt for Austria wonderkid Oghenetejiri Adejenughure

arts.ttl: Winning FA Cup is 'not going to change' Man City's 'poor' season - Guardiola
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says that winning the FA Cup and qualifying for the Champions League this season would be a "huge success" but not change the reality that they have had a "poor" campaign.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth v Manchester City: FA Cup quarter-final updates
arts.sum: Minute-by-minute coverage of 4.30pm BST kick-off Email Tim with your thoughts on the game Afternoon everyone and welcome to the last and biggest of this year’s FA Cup quarter-finals. The last time Manchester City went to Bournemouth, on 2 November, the result was that the tectonic plates of English football began to shift. City were top of the league with seven wins from nine games, while Bournemouth were muddling along in mid-table. But it was Bournemouth who won the match, 2-1, and Liverpool who took over at the top as City fell from their pedestal like a deposed dictator. They lost their next three games and never looked forward. From that day to this, City have scraped just seven more league wins and have been the 13th-best team in the land – even worse than Man United. Continue reading...
